title: tengine.silo.verify v1
description: Fires when verification_report.json appears in a silo session dir — the run completed cleanly. Subset of the full report (which can be 100KB+); session_dir holds the full artifact set for deep-dives. Producer: the silo-watcher nervous-bus adapter (~/.config/nervous-bus/adapters/silo-watcher/watcher.py::build_verify_event, systemd --user unit nervous-silo-watcher.service — confirmed running as of 2026-07-02), NOT tengine's own EventChannel registry; this is an out-of-repo directory-poll adapter that lives in the private overlay config tree. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-02 (ecosystem-audit-p2): an earlier audit pass flagged this schema as dead/orphaned with a mismatched Android consumer (apps/android HearthViewModel.kt ~line 8848). That flag does not hold up under direct inspection: the producer is active and this schema's field names (silo, avg_fps, min_fps, anomaly_count) match both the live payload AND the Android consumer, which reads 'silo_name' with a fallback to 'silo' — so the consumer resolves correctly against this producer today. Flagging here only so a human can double-check this conclusion; no functional fix was needed.

success.description: verification.success from the report — overall pass/fail.

message.description: verification.message — one-line human summary (e.g. '6 PASS, 3 FAIL, 1 WARN (Avg: 12.0 FPS)').

top_anomaly_codes.description: First N anomaly codes from analysis.anomalies (truncated; full list in session_dir).
